reasoning: FIDE ratings are published globally on the first of each month and generally remain unchanged until the next month. The Wikipedia page 'FIDE rankings' mirrors these official top lists. Since the current rating is 2463 (as of February 2026), reaching a rating 1% higher would require a jump of 25 Elo points to 2488. Gaining 25 points in a single month is extremely difficult for an established 2400+ player, as their K-factor is only 10 (requiring a score of +2.5 over expected, typically achievable only with a spectacular performance). For the first resolution date (2026-02-22), the March FIDE list will not have been published yet, meaning her rating on the Wikipedia page is essentially certain to remain exactly the same as today. For the second date (2026-03-17), the March 2026 FIDE update will be out. Considering the high threshold to gain 25 points in one standard classical chess month, along with the lack of evidential catalyst that she is posting a breakout performance recently (having slipped to #20 in the world), the probabilities for both an immediate increase and a substantial jump next month are very low. The probabilities are kept close to the lowest allowable bounds (0.02 and 0.05) to reflect these practical and statistical near-impossibilities.
